apiOverview.title: API Overview
apiOverview.description: InTransparency provides RESTful APIs for universities to push verified student data, for companies to search and filter candidates by real skills, and for third-party platforms to build integrations. All data is institution-verified and GDPR compliant.

students.title: Students API
students.description: Manage student profiles, projects, grades, and verified credentials. Push data from your student information system or let students self-register.

search.title: Search API
search.description: Search candidates by skills, grades, location, and project type. AI-powered matching ranks results by relevance to your requirements.

placements.title: Placements API
placements.description: Track and report placement outcomes. Get real-time data on views, contacts, interviews, and hires for your institution's graduates.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Receive real-time notifications for events like new candidate matches, profile updates, placement milestones, and verification completions.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: All API requests are authenticated using API keys. Each key is scoped to your organization and can be configured with granular permissions. Keys are managed from your dashboard settings.
steps: Generate an API key from your organization dashboard

Use separate keys for development and production environments

hero.title: Transparency Changes Everything.
hero.description: Across every sector — tech, engineering, business, science, design — the way we hire is broken. Skills are claimed, not proven. Talent is hidden behind résumés. We're building the trust layer that fixes this, starting in Europe.

items.2: Rewards good writers, not good builders

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: We're building the European standard for transparent hiring — where every person, in every sector, is seen for what they can actually do. Not for what they claim on a résumé. Not for where they studied. For the work they've done, verified by the institutions that taught them.
mission.quote: "The future of work is transparent. Every skill proven, every hire confident, every career earned."

paragraphs.0: Europe has a paradox: companies can't find the right talent, and graduates can't find jobs. Both sides lose because the system between them — résumés, job boards, opaque algorithms — was never designed for trust.
paragraphs.1: InTransparency exists because we believe transparency solves this. When a student's work is verified by their university, a recruiter doesn't need to guess. When a company shows clear requirements, a student doesn't need to decode jargon. When outcomes are tracked, universities can prove their impact.
paragraphs.2: We started in Bergamo with one university partnership. The vision is European: a continent where hiring is based on proof, not promises. Where a mechanical engineer from Brescia and a data scientist from Berlin compete on the same transparent playing field.
paragraphs.3: This is not a job board. This is the trust layer for the future of work.

0.title: Skills Over Stories
0.description: What you've built matters more than how you describe it. A verified project is worth a thousand résumé bullet points — in every discipline.

1.title: Access Over Gatekeeping
1.description: Every student deserves visibility — not just the top 5% from elite schools. Talent is everywhere. Opportunity shouldn't be.

2.title: Transparency Over Opacity
2.description: Students see who views their work. Companies see verified data. Universities see real outcomes. No hidden algorithms, no black boxes.

3.title: Proof Over Promises
3.description: We don't ask candidates to prove themselves with words. Their institution-verified work speaks for itself — across tech, engineering, business, and every sector.

killResume.description: Resumes were invented in 1482. Today we have AI, verified skill analysis, and academic verification. Why are we still relying on a 500-year-old format to hire for 21st-century roles?

0.q: Is it really free for students?
0.a: Yes. Portfolio, assessments, job search, contract check — everything is included. No credit card, no hidden costs. Students never pay.

1.q: What do companies get for free?
1.a: Unlimited search across all verified profiles, 3 talent unlocks at signup, 1 job posting, and basic pipeline management. No credit card, no trial expiration.

2.q: Why is the Growth plan worth it?
2.a: At €149/month, you get 50 talent unlocks, 10 job posts, and full access to AI hiring tools — including the Hiring Advisor and Interview Kit Generator. That's less than LinkedIn Recruiter Lite, with university-verified data.

3.a: No. The entire platform is free for all educational institutions — universities, schools, ITS, training centers. No student limits, no feature limits, no hidden costs.

4.q: Is there a contract or lock-in?
4.a: No. Monthly billing, cancel anytime. No annual contracts unless you choose one (annual plans save 2 months). Your data is always exportable.

5.q: How is this different from LinkedIn or Handshake?
5.a: Every profile is backed by university-verified projects and grades — not self-reported claims. You see actual work before you contact anyone. And universities get the platform free, while Handshake charges $1,000–$20,000/year.

0.question: Is it free for universities?
0.answer: Yes. The core platform is free for all academic partners. Premium features like embeddable widgets and custom branding are available for institutions that want deeper integration.

1.question: How do we verify student projects?
1.answer: Through a simple dashboard. Review submitted projects, add grades and ECTS credits, and approve with one click. Batch verification is available for large cohorts.

2.question: What data do we get?
2.answer: Real-time placement analytics — which companies view, contact, and hire your students. Skills gap analysis, curriculum alignment scores, and exportable reports.

3.question: How do students join?
3.answer: Students can self-register or you can bulk-import them via CSV. They create their portfolio, and you verify their work through the dashboard.

4.question: Is it GDPR compliant?
4.answer: Fully compliant. Students control their own data, consent is managed per-feature, and all data is stored in EU data centers.

hero.title: Making the Path from Education to Career
hero.description: We believe that talent should be recognized through real, verified skills. InTransparency connects students, institutions, and companies in a transparent marketplace where everyone wins.

0.label: Internships activated every year in Italy

1.label: Interns who don't continue with the company

paragraphs.0: Every year, hundreds of thousands of young Italians start internship programs hoping to build a career. However, the current system doesn't always manage to recognize the skills acquired during education, creating a gap between training and job opportunities.
paragraphs.1: There is often a missing link between what students can do and what companies are looking for. Without proper tools to surface verified competencies, talent risks going unnoticed.
paragraphs.2: Universities and training institutions, despite investing in quality education, often lack the tools to monitor employment outcomes for their students. This lack of data makes it difficult to improve the system.

0.title: Skills Come First
0.description: A student's value should be measured by what they can do — verified projects, real competencies, institutional endorsements — not just by their educational path.

1.title: Investing in Talent Discovery
1.description: Companies looking for qualified candidates deserve effective tools to find them. A system based on verified skills makes the process more efficient for everyone.

2.title: Connecting Education and Work
2.description: We believe universities and companies can collaborate better. When student competencies are visible and verified, matching becomes natural and faster.

3.title: Transparency Improves the System
3.description: When placement data is visible, when skills are verified and outcomes are tracked, every actor in the system can make better decisions.

0.title: Transparency in Every Interaction
0.description: Every interaction on our platform is traceable. Companies see verified skills. Students see who's interested. Institutions see outcomes.

1.title: Verified Skills, Real Projects
1.description: We believe a 30/30 thesis project tells a lot about a candidate. Our platform puts concrete, institution-verified work first.

2.title: A Fair System for Everyone
2.description: We're building a marketplace where value goes to those who create it. Every actor — students, universities, companies — finds a sustainable and transparent model.

3.title: Meritocracy Based on Real Projects
3.description: No name-brand university bias. Your verified projects speak for themselves. The best work wins, regardless of background.

cta.subtitle: Join a growing community where talent is recognized, skills are verified, and the right connections happen naturally.

perScuoleSuperiori.faq1Q: How does it work for PCTO?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq1A: Students upload projects completed during PCTO (internships, traineeships, company activities). Teachers verify with institutional badge. Companies search for students with specific skills and contact them directly for internships or first jobs.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq2Q: How much does it cost for the school?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq2A: The basic service is completely free for schools. Companies pay to contact students. Enterprise options available for institutions with advanced needs.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq3Q: Does it work for alternanza scuola-lavoro too?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq3A: Yes, the platform supports all forms of work experience: PCTO, curricular internships, training placements, and first work experiences. Every activity can be documented and verified by the school.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq4A: Students upload projects and teachers verify them through the dashboard. Verification adds an institutional badge that increases the student's visibility and credibility in the marketplace.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq5Q: Can we use it for RAV?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq5A: Yes, the analytics dashboard provides exportable data useful for the Self-Assessment Report: placement metrics, most in-demand skills, companies that contacted students.
perScuoleSuperiori.faq6Q: Can licei use the platform too?
perScuoleSuperiori.faq6A: Absolutely yes. Licei also have the requirement of 90 PCTO hours. The platform helps document the transversal skills acquired and connect students with educational and job opportunities.

forItsInstitutes.faq1A: Companies browse verified graduates and proactively reach out to students with matching skills. Students don't apply - they get discovered. Your institutional verification gives them a competitive edge.
forItsInstitutes.faq2A: Core features are included at no cost. Companies full access. Enterprise features available for larger institutions.
forItsInstitutes.faq3A: Students manually upload projects and you verify them through our simple dashboard. For larger institutions, we can set up API integration (contact us for details). Zero IT work for you after setup.
forItsInstitutes.faq4Q: Can we add Career Day tools?
forItsInstitutes.faq4A: Yes! Optional Premium Embed add-on (€500/year) includes Career Day platform with QR codes, interview booking, and branded widgets. But core marketplace is free with no hidden costs. See pricing page for details.

forItsInstitutes.faq6Q: What if students don't get discovered?
forItsInstitutes.faq6A: Analytics show you early warnings: "Student X: 0 views in 30 days - skills mismatch?" You can intervene early, update skills/projects, or offer career counseling. Prevention vs reaction.

faq.sa1: You can register for free by clicking 'Register' and selecting 'Student'. You'll need to provide your name, email, and create a password. After registration, you can complete your profile and connect your institution.
faq.sq2: How do I connect my institution?
faq.sa2: From your dashboard, go to 'University Integration' and click 'Connect University'. Search for your university or ITS, enter your institutional email, and verify your account. Once verified, your grades and projects can be synced automatically.
faq.sq3: Are my projects visible to everyone?
faq.sa3: No, you have complete control over your profile visibility. You can choose to make your portfolio public or private. You can also select which projects to show and which to hide.
faq.sq4: How does the referral program work?
faq.sa4: Invite your friends using your referral link. For every 3 friends who register, you get 1 month of Premium for free. With 10 referrals you get 6 months, and with 50 referrals you get lifetime Premium!
faq.sq5: How much does InTransparency cost for students?
faq.sa5: InTransparency is completely free for students! We also offer optional Premium plans with advanced features like in-depth AI analysis and greater visibility.
faq.ca1: You can use our advanced search to filter candidates by skills, university, graduation year, GPA, and more. Our AI matching system automatically suggests the most suitable candidates for your open positions.
faq.cq2: How much does it cost to contact a candidate?
faq.ca2: With the Pay-per-Contact plan, each contact costs €5. If you contact more than 20 candidates per month, we recommend the Enterprise plan at €99/month which includes unlimited contacts, advanced filters, and API access.
faq.ca3: Our algorithm analyzes students' projects, verified skills, university grades, and preferences to calculate a compatibility score. The higher the score, the better the match with your needs.
faq.ca4: Yes! You can post job listings from your dashboard. Students can apply directly and you'll receive notifications for each new application.
faq.ia1: Institutions can register for free and get a dedicated dashboard to monitor their students' placement, see which companies are interested, and access detailed analytics.
faq.ia2: The embeddable widget (€500/year) allows you to integrate InTransparency directly into your careers portal. It shows real-time matches and increases student registrations by an average of 40%.
faq.ia3: Absolutely. We are GDPR compliant and students always have control over their data. Institutions can only see aggregate statistics, not individual personal data without consent.
faq.pq1: What payment methods do you accept?
faq.pa1: We accept all major credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express), PayPal, and bank transfer for Enterprise plans.
faq.pa2: Yes, you can cancel at any time from the Subscription section of your dashboard. The subscription will remain active until the end of the current billing period.
faq.pa3: We offer a full refund within 14 days of purchase if you are not satisfied with the service. Contact support to request a refund.
faq.prq1: How do you protect my data?
faq.pra1: We use end-to-end encryption, secure servers in Europe, and follow security best practices. We are GDPR compliant and never sell your data to third parties.
faq.pra2: Yes, you can request complete deletion of your account and all associated data at any time from your profile settings or by contacting support.
faq.pra3: You have complete control. You can choose to make your profile public, visible only to verified companies, or completely private. You can also block specific companies.

guide.title: Italian Contract Types — Plain Language Guide
guide.subtitle: Every contract type explained: what it means, what protections you get, and when it's appropriate.

tempo_indeterminato.description: The gold standard. Permanent employment with full protections. The employer needs a justified reason to fire you. You get TFR, sick leave, vacation, overtime pay, and unemployment benefits if dismissed.

tempo_determinato.description: Same protections as permanent, but with an end date. Can be renewed, but total duration cannot exceed 24 months. After that, it must become permanent or end.

apprendistato.description: A real job with a training component. Great for young workers — the company gets tax breaks, you get mentorship and full employment rights. Must include a formal training plan. Can pay up to 2 levels below standard.

stage.description: An extracurricular internship. Not a real employment contract — it's a training agreement. Must be paid in most regions (€300-€800/month). Should involve genuine learning, not cheap labor. Max 6 months.

tirocinio.description: A curricular internship tied to your degree program. Can be unpaid if part of your curriculum. Your university must be involved as a guarantor. Learning objectives must be defined.

partita_iva.description: Self-employment. You are your own boss — you invoice clients, pay your own taxes and INPS. No employment protections. Your effective income is roughly 50-60% of what you invoice after taxes and contributions.

cococo.description: A collaboration contract. You're not an employee but work in a coordinated way with the company. Some pension contributions are paid. Very few protections. Often misused to avoid employment obligations.

somministrazione.description: You're employed by an agency but work at another company. Same pay and protections as direct employees. The agency handles payroll. Good way to get a foot in the door at large companies.
somministrazione.bestFor: Entry into large companies, temp roles

collaborazione_occasionale.description: Occasional, one-off work. Max €5,000/year per client. No employment relationship. Very limited — if it becomes regular, it should be a different contract type.

redFlags.title: Common Red Flags in Job Offers
redFlags.subtitle: Patterns to watch for when evaluating a contract. If you see these, ask questions before signing.

0.title: "We'll start with a trial period... unpaid"
0.description: Italian law does not allow unpaid trial periods for employment contracts. The trial period (periodo di prova) is part of the contract — you must be paid from day one. An unpaid 'trial' is illegal.
0.action: Ask for the contract in writing before starting. If they refuse to pay during the trial, this is a violation of labor law. Contact a sindacato.

1.title: "Open a Partita IVA and we'll give you a fixed monthly fee"
1.description: If a company asks you to be freelance but gives you fixed hours, works you on-site, and treats you like an employee — this is 'false P.IVA' (falsa partita IVA). It's illegal under Italian law since 2015.
1.action: If you have fixed hours, work on-site, use company tools, and can't take other clients — you're an employee. Ask for a real contract. A labor judge can reclassify it.

2.title: "The CCNL doesn't apply here" or no mention of CCNL
2.description: Almost all Italian companies must apply a CCNL (national collective agreement). This sets your minimum pay, vacation days, sick leave, and more. If they say CCNL doesn't apply, they may be trying to avoid minimum protections.
2.action: Ask which CCNL applies and at which livello. Verify the minimum pay on sindacato websites. If they refuse to specify, that's a red flag.

3.title: Stage that lasts longer than 6 months
3.description: Extracurricular internships (stage) have a legal maximum of 6 months in most Italian regions. If a company offers a 12-month stage, they may be using internship rules to avoid a real contract.
3.action: After 6 months, you should either be hired or the internship should end. A repeated or extended stage for the same tasks is a sign of exploitation.

4.title: "You'll start as intern, then we'll see about hiring"
4.description: Vague promises of future employment are a common way to keep you as cheap labor. There's no legal obligation to hire after an internship ends, and many companies don't.
4.action: Ask for the conversion rate — what percentage of interns get hired? Ask for it in writing. If they won't commit, assume the internship is all you'll get.

5.title: Pay significantly below CCNL minimum
5.description: Each CCNL sets minimum pay per livello. If you're offered less than the minimum for your role and classification, the company is violating the collective agreement. This happens more often than you'd think.
5.action: Look up your CCNL and livello minimums. Compare with the offer. If it's below, negotiate or contact a sindacato — they can help recover the difference.

6.title: "Sign this NDA before we show you the contract"
6.description: While NDAs for proprietary information are normal, an NDA that prevents you from discussing your pay or contract terms is a red flag. Pay transparency is your right.
6.action: Read the NDA carefully. You always have the right to discuss your contract with a sindacato, a lawyer, or labor inspectors. An NDA cannot override this.

redFlags.unionTitle: When in doubt, ask a sindacato
redFlags.unionDescription: Italian labor unions offer free contract review and legal advice. They can check your contract, recover unpaid wages, and represent you in disputes. Membership costs €5-15/month.
